active_ingredients: Meclizine hydrochloride 12.5 mg, 25 mg chewable/tablets (OTC for motion sickness, e.g., Bonine; Rx Antivert for vertigo)
indications_and_usage: Uses: prevents and treats the nausea, vomiting, and dizziness associated with motion sickness; by prescription it is also used for the management of vertigo associated with diseases affecting the vestibular system. It is an antihistamine (antiemetic) with a longer duration and somewhat less drowsiness than dimenhydrinate.
contraindications: Do not use if you are allergic to meclizine or related antihistamines; use caution rather than an absolute contraindication in narrow-angle glaucoma, enlarged prostate/urinary retention, and severe asthma.
warnings_and_cautions: May cause marked drowsiness - do not drive or operate machinery until you know how it affects you; avoid alcohol and other sedatives. Be careful in older adults (confusion, falls, urinary retention, dry mouth). Ask a doctor before use if you have glaucoma, breathing problems such as emphysema or chronic bronchitis, or difficulty urinating due to an enlarged prostate. May cause dizziness on its own.
adverse_reactions: Drowsiness, dry mouth, blurred vision, headache, fatigue; rarely excitation in children.
drug_interactions: Alcohol, sedatives, tranquilizers, sleep aids, and other CNS depressants (additive sedation); other anticholinergic drugs (additive dry mouth, constipation, urinary retention).
dosage_and_administration: Motion sickness (adults and children 12 and over): take 25-50 mg about 1 hour before travel; repeat every 24 hours as needed for the duration of travel. Vertigo (by prescription): 25-100 mg daily in divided doses. Children under 12: ask a doctor.
overdosage: Overdose causes drowsiness or, in children, paradoxical excitement, hallucinations, and seizures; anticholinergic signs (dry mouth, dilated pupils, flushing, fast heartbeat, urinary retention). Seek medical help; contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
